summary: Adversarial skeptical re-judge of each ceremony against a strict load-bearing bar

result.reason: The Challenger changed the converged recommendation in substance. Generator's opening (R1): "opt-in is right" — a flat opt-in where the minimal tier is treated as safely optional. Converged answer: "neither pure opt-in nor pure mandatory — synthesis: mandatory fail-CLOSED identity contract + opt-in implementation," adding a NEW mandatory floor in every install plus a paired requirement that the Round Table stubs default to deny. The driver was a concrete, specific defect the Generator missed: the minimal tier's identity stubs ($rt_is_admin / $rt_user_resolver, line 113) run auth=open|none and thus FAIL OPEN — unresolved identity is authorized — a secure-defaults inversion that would plausibly have shipped under the proposed flat opt-in. The R2 exchange distinguishes this from relabeling (stub flips open→deny, a real behavioral change on an existing seam). This is both a real risk caught and a substantive scope change to what the parent should DO (rewrite 14.2; carry versioned-not-vendored propagation as a 14.4 dependency).

Genuine /spar ceremony with a Generator opening proposal that was substantively changed by the Challenger. BEFORE (Generator R1): floor item "throttle/lockout" — i.e. hard per-account lockout as a baseline control. AFTER (converged baseline, item 1): "Per-IP throttle + per-account exponential backoff (delay, not denial; resets on success). Not hard lockout — that's a DoS footgun against low-entropy cohort usernames (CWE-307)." A security control present in the opening was reversed/replaced on the Challenger's specific CWE-307 argument; the report's own wrap-up confirms it "removed a control the obvious reading would've added (hard lockout)." Additionally, the Generator's "admin-reseed replaces email reset" had no base case for a sole admin locking themselves out (CWE-640); the Challenger surfaced this and the converged form added a "host-shell break-glass" last-resort recovery path to close an otherwise-unrecoverable bootstrap-lockout gap. These change what gets built (a reversed control, an added recovery path), not merely wording or citations.

This is a genuine ceremony turn (Generator "Tessa" SPARK round 2 responding to Challenger "Rosetta", with explicit SIGNAL {agree:false}). The Challenger surfaced specific factual overclaims that the Generator conceded and acted on, not mere polish. Most concretely, the OVERCLAIM FLAGS section: 'LOOM "builds the rest of this page" -- conceded, it's an overclaim. This page is hand-written PHP with no build step; Loom builds and runs the *products*, not this HTML.' As a direct result, 'Round-1 candidate #2 is **retired**' and replaced with verifiable alternatives ("The system behind everything we ship." / "It runs the products in our portfolio -- today."). That is a specific factual error in a candidate marketing tagline (claiming Loom builds a page it does not build) that would plausibly have shipped on a live portfolio page — exactly the kind of false claim the project's "load-bearing honesty" value exists to prevent. A second concrete honesty catch: SPARRING "Honest answers FROM AI" was conceded as implying a correctness guarantee the framework does not make, forcing the mechanism-preserving rephrase ("...even when it would rather please you"). These are substantive corrections to what the copy would claim, not framing or rigor-signalling. Confidence is medium rather than high because we only see the Generator's self-report of Rosetta's flags, not Rosetta's verbatim output, and because a SPARK divergent phase might have filtered the overclaim in the later Cut anyway.

The file is a single task-notification fragment ("Rosetta — SPARK expand"), not a full Generator/Challenger run: there is no visible Generator opening proposal and no converged recommendation, so no before/after substance change can be established. The only concrete contribution is the agent signaling agree:false to keep the SPARK phase open for one more expand pass, on the grounds that the "felt-stakes" rewrite was applied to SPARRING copy and the Loom tagline but not to Loom's body bullets ("LLM model routing," "synced skills library," "coordination boards"). That is additive wording/option polish to marketing copy on a webpage (e.g. suggesting "each task goes to the best model so you don't pay for the wrong one") — a stylistic jargon-consistency observation, not an error, bug, risk, or decision reversal. It is saturation/rigor signalling about whether to iterate once more, not something that changes what the parent should DO. No defect that would plausibly have shipped is caught.

The artifact is a single SPARK round-1 task-notification (Tessa, "final expand pass") whose entire content is rewording website marketing copy from jargon to "felt stakes" phrasing — e.g. "Reusable agent personas, coordination boards, and orchestration built in" rewritten to "A standing crew of specialist AI agents... that hand work between them." That is presentation/framing/wording of promotional taglines, not a material decision change. No converged recommendation is shown shifting from a prior proposal; the two real decisions ("agentic" site-wide defrost, keep-vs-replace "ceremony") are explicitly deferred to a later phase and left unresolved. The only accuracy-flavored point (defrost "coordination boards" downward because the site runs the persona layer only) is the generator's own copy choice about marketing-blurb precision, not a challenger-surfaced defect that would have shipped harm. Even read as a real ceremony, the Challenger/expand contribution is wording, not substance.

The captured artifact is a single task-notification — one agent's ("Rosetta — SPARK expand") final SPARK closing signal — i.e., a status/signal note, not a captured Generator/Challenger pressure-test we can evaluate for a converged-vs-opening decision change. The only concrete substantive item is a marketing-copy overclaim correction ("coordination boards... built in" → defrosted, because project-context line 34 says rf runs "persona layer only"), and Rosetta explicitly credits that catch to the OTHER role: "Good catch by the Generator." So no Challenger contribution producing a material decision change is visible in this fragment. The rest of the signal is saturation-checking taglines/bullets and ratifying convergence — wording/option-coverage, not substance. No before/after converged recommendation differing from an opening proposal is present.

The PNP pass returned {agree: false}, refusing convergence to catch a specific, source-grounded factual overclaim in shipped website copy. Loom bullet 4 read "A standing crew of reusable specialist agents that coordinate across projects." The Challenger grounded this against project-context line 34 (rf runs the persona layer ONLY — in-session personas, coordination through Bart, no agent-runtime, no autonomous coordination) and showed that, because the card asserts "how WE build our products" + "all of them run on it today," the bullet claims rf's actual practice. "Standing crew" falsely implies a persistent runtime; "coordinate across projects" falsely implies autonomous coordination. The prior passes (the Cut + earlier rounds) had explicitly policed this same autonomy-overclaim family elsewhere (tagline distillation, "coordination boards") yet let bullet 4 through, so it would plausibly have shipped. Concrete before/after with a named fix: "A reusable set of specialist agent personas, shared across projects." This is a grounded accuracy defect, not wording polish.

The Challenger (Rosetta) caught a concrete factual overclaim on public-facing rf brand-card copy. Feature bullet 4 originally implied a "standing crew" (persistent agent runtime) of agents that "coordinate across projects" (autonomy). The Generator (Tessa) concedes this is false against the actual deployment: project-context.md:34 states "persona layer only, coordination through Bart, no agent-runtime," and Tessa admits "I verified the bullet against Loom-the-product's capability and not against rf's actual deployment... 'Standing crew' implies a persistent runtime and 'coordinate across projects' implies autonomy; neither is true here. Conceded." The converged bullet became "Reusable specialist agent personas, shared across projects," dropping the runtime and autonomy claims. This is a specific capability overclaim on shipping brand copy that would plausibly have gone live otherwise — not wording polish, since the before-state asserted a product capability that does not exist and the after-state asserts only what is verifiable.

The closing PNP artifact documents the Challenger catching a specific overclaim in shippable marketing copy: Loom card "bullet 4" used "standing" and "coordinate" language, implying persistent-runtime, autonomously-coordinating agents. The Challenger flagged this as false against project-context line 34 — the reality is a persona layer only, in-session, with no autonomous coordination or persistent runtime. The converged copy dropped both words to "Reusable specialist agent personas, shared across projects," and the Challenger explicitly refused to let the claim be rescued by rescoping to "Loom-the-product," which would have reintroduced the rf-usage-vs-product-capability ambiguity. This is a concrete capability misrepresentation in public-facing copy that would plausibly have shipped — not hedging or citation polish. The tagline/"how-we" voice note in the same artifact IS just register polish (explicitly non-blocking, "Bart's call"), but the bullet-4 fix is a genuine accuracy catch. Confidence is medium because the evidence is the Challenger's self-report in the closing note rather than a visible before/after diff of the Generator's opening proposal.

The Generator's opening (R1) recommended pure "keep-and-fix" — keep Case B and fix it with a 5-rule clarity/stakes-legibility pass (a wording/readability fix). The Challenger, from a divergent survey-methodology evidence base, surfaced a structural cross-case comparability asymmetry the clarity pass "cannot reach" (Case B mandates 5 sub-population analyses + contradiction-hold + failed-replication discount + moderator-gated effect size vs. Case A's single undifferentiated recommendation), and proposed a third path: a pre-registered cross-case load covariate. This caused the Generator in R2 to "withdraw pure keep-and-fix" and adopt Option (b), adding a pre-registered cross-case load covariate (per-case Krippendorff's α divergence on C3/C4), two dropout riders, and human case-order counterbalancing — none of which were in the opening. The parent explicitly confirms the substance shifted to the Challenger-introduced material: "the value here is concentrated in the methodology riders, not the wording fix... they're the real work" and "I'd do B first — it's the load-bearing change." This is an added option family and a real scope change to the analysis-plan deliverable, not polish or caveats. Confidence is medium because the underlying pre-reg line citations cannot be independently verified from this artifact alone.

The Generator (Theo) opened with the decision already settled: "Yes -- this is worthy of submission" with the full strategy (arXiv-cs.MA preprint + GitHub release + practitioner blog + workshop submission; don't lead with HN cold). The converged answer is identical in substance: "Yes, the framework is submission-worthy... arXiv preprint + GitHub release + practitioner blog now-ready; workshops after editing pass; main-track after Phase 3 data." The Challenger (Anya) explicitly "largely concurs." No reversal, no replaced approach, no dropped/added option family at the decision level. Anya's contributions were: (a) second-evidence-base confirmation of novelty with more citations (Park, Shanahan, Sharma, Mitchell, Gebru), (b) three extra items added to the contribution INVENTORY (B1 D8/D9, B2 ceiling-hit detector, B3 distribution-shape) which don't change what Bart should DO, and (c) document-edit refinements appended to an editing checklist (C2 judge calibration, C4 1-3→5-point scale, C5 audience narrowing, C6 taxonomy). The strongest candidate — the converged "single highest-leverage edit" (C1, the proposed-empirical-evaluation subsection using Sharma's preference-flip method) — addresses a gap Theo had ALREADY named in his concerns 3 and 5 and explicitly routed to the alignment Challenger by design; Anya supplied the concrete wording for a pre-identified gap, which is refinement, not a reversed call. There is also no "would have shipped" error: this is a meta-review producing pre-submission advice on a not-yet-submitted document, so every item is improve-before-submitting polish. Net: the Challenger made the review more thorough via citations, an enriched contribution list, and a longer edit checklist, but the converged recommendation does not differ in substance from the Generator's opening proposal.

The Challenger (Renata, safety/sociotechnical lineage) produced eight elaborately-cited residual gaps (Leveson/Vaughan/Dekker/Hollnagel/Reason), but the document's own post-ceremony partner-review retracts seven of eight as scope-violations and keeps only G8 as "an optional enhancement, not a gap" (the optional `sync-loom --verify`). The genuinely actionable finding — charter §5.10 "Loom is built to be extended" — came from PARTNER pushback, explicitly NOT the Challenger: "It did not surface during the formal four-phase ceremony because the in-ceremony Challenger's professional lineage had no vocabulary for..." The meta-finding concedes "the Challenger's evidence base... was not scope-appropriate for the system under test." The Challenger's only durable effect on the converged set was cutting E5 at PNP (before: Hayley's PNP Round 1 listed E5 surviving; after: "E5... was cut at PNP"), but its "substance folds into E1 and E2" — a trivial pruning of a deferred list of essay candidates, not a change to what the parent should do. No real error/risk was caught; the Challenger instead introduced scope-creep the partner had to refute. The extensive failure-mode citation apparatus is rigor-signaling that did not change the substance of the decision.

The captured file is only the Challenger-prompt for "The Cut Round 2" — instructions to Renata plus a summary of Hayley's proposed Cut — and ends with "Pressure-test, then sign." It contains NO Challenger response: no reasoning, no retention/cut decisions, and none of the required sign-off lines (e.g. `RENATA-CUT-CLOSE: agree/with adjustments/disagree`). The three items teed up for scrutiny (B4 alarm fatigue, B5 dry-run burden, H4 trust-gestures) are merely posed as questions, not adjudicated. With the Challenger's actual output absent, there is no converged recommendation to compare against an opening proposal, no demonstrated decision change, and no specific error caught. As captured this is a prompt/spec fragment, not a completed Generator/Challenger exchange, so load-bearing impact cannot be established.

The Challenger demonstrably changed the converged recommendation away from the Generator's committed approach, not just polish. At the Cut, the committed synthesis B still extrapolated that the shared waist carries agent reply-addressing and "holds only with an additive reply_to/correlation_id field" in the envelope. The Challenger's PNP stress-test showed that additive field smuggles the full EIP Request-Reply pattern (Return Address, Correlation Identifier, Dead-Letter, two-sided idempotency) and collides with D43's per-project copy-propagation cost (a contract change propagating by hand-merge across diverged copies). This forced Gen R2 to reverse to "two independent one-way fire-and-forget lanes over the existing envelope" with NO envelope field added, and to explicitly defer Request-Reply plus document a named tripwire. Before: "additive correlation_id field"; after: "no shared-contract field is added… dissolves the D43 collision." Separately, the Challenger's Conway's-Law/zero-shared-commits test reversed the doc's "fold runtime into core-agents" to "separate package behind a port." Both are substantive reversals of the doc's defaults, not caveats. Confidence is medium because the artifact self-narrates both specialists and the cited Loom/Phase-13 line references could not be independently verified.

Genuine Generator/Challenger run that materially changed all three §13 capstone claims. 4-A reversed: Generator opened "A SPARRING ceremony artifact IS an ADR"; converged result is "NOT an ADR... the rationale dossier an ADR distills from" — driven by the Challenger showing ADR content is prior art (Tyree & Akerman 2005, MADR, QOC, IBIS/gIBIS) plus the append-only-one-pager vs. multi-round-transcript structural mismatch. 4-B demoted from a full sub-arc to a one-paragraph contrast, with two specific factual errors struck: the false "no evidence gate" claim (Challenger: prototype-and-test IS an evidence gate) and "loudest voice" claim (Challenger: DFV/dot-voting ARE anti-loudest-voice rubrics; Liedtka HBR 2018). 4-C restructured from "the gate-based enforcement layer beneath all five frameworks" to "four structurally different relationships" plus the uncredited-genus insight (SPARRING is a variant within institutionalized dialectic — Mason 1969, Schweiger et al. 1986, Heuer — not the substrate). These are substantive reversals and the removal of factually wrong claims that would otherwise have shipped in the framework doc, not polish or citation-padding.

The Generator's opening (R1) accepted the draft's central premise verbatim: "Torres / CDH / OST + RAT is gap-free under strict human discipline" as agent mediation increases, three SPARRING disciplines "move from 'good practice that mature trios already approximate' to 'structurally required.'" The Generator only quibbled about expanding the D2/D3/D4 triad to include D7. The Challenger, using a distinct evidence base (Torres CDH Ch. 5/7, Cagan SVPG "fake agile discovery"/"passenger trios"), refuted the gap-free premise — Torres documents the same failures under all-human discipline — and showed D6/D7 are disciplines PM practice already names, not SPARRING additions. The Generator conceded "fully" and called the citations "decisive." The converged claim is materially different: from "SPARRING closes a gap that opens when agents arrive" to "SPARRING is the gate-based enforcement of disciplines PM-practice frameworks already name as ritually-required, which degrade under BOTH organizational pressure and agent mediation." The parent recommendation concretely instructs dropping "gap-free under strict human discipline," reframing the §4 table from competitor-vs-competitor to "ritual approximation vs gate-based enforcement," and changing "structural" to "gate-based" — before extracting public Series 2 LinkedIn posts. The opening single-specialist pass did not catch the gap-free overstatement; the Challenger's distinct evidence base was required. This is a substantive, quotable before/after on a publication-bound claim, not mere wording polish.

Real spar with genuinely disjoint evidence bases (internal corpus vs. external decision-science literature confirmed to contain zero overlapping refs). The Generator's opening proposal kept the central claim "SPARRING is [the premortem's] enforced generalization," asking only for cosmetic tightenings. The Challenger surfaced a concrete, citable error: the premortem's empirically-validated active ingredient is prospective hindsight / certainty framing (Mitchell, Russo & Pennington 1989; Kahneman 2011), NOT adversariality — so the opening claim "silently dropped the cognitive mechanism" and asserted inaccurate genealogy. This forced a substantive reversal in the converged wording: from "SPARRING is its enforced generalization" to "SPARRING does NOT generalize that cognitive mechanism; it keeps the premortem's social licensing and replaces the single-room exercise with gate-based enforcement of disciplines that adversarial collaboration (Tetlock 2015; Kahneman & Klein 2009) and ACH (Heuer 1999) already name." The threshold attribution also flipped (independence+grounding leads; "no stake" demoted to an agent-specific amplifier). The Generator's internal corpus had no rebuttal, so absent the Challenger this mis-attributed genealogy would have shipped into the framework doc. This is a material change to the substance of the decided artifact, not wording polish.

The Challenger demonstrably narrowed the central claim's scope and caught a reversal risk the opening pass would have shipped. BEFORE (Generator §11.6): "SPARRING is the gate-based enforcement of validated learning." AFTER (converged §11.6): "SPARRING gate-enforces the pivot-or-persevere convergence — not validated learning as a whole... SPARRING must not gate that loop: Lean Startup is a speed doctrine, and a both-must-agree ceremony at loop frequency would violate it." This is a real scope change, not wording polish. Distinct from the Generator's own Round-1 self-concessions (genealogy errors), the Challenger raised three new substantive defects: (1) the speed/scope reversal — gating the BML loop would violate Lean Startup's "learn faster" engine, forcing scope down to the low-frequency pivot cadence; (2) the D2 over-claim ("Lean Startup has NO distinct-evidence-base discipline / one shared dashboard") was factually false per Blank's get-out-of-the-building, corrected to "no independent second interpreter"; (3) the escalation fix requires an explicit independence precondition (folie a deux / groupthink) or it is "theater with two signatures." These changed what gets written into the framework doc. Caveat: the artifact is self-reported and the domain is the framework auditing its own claim text, but the before/after is concrete and verifiable in-document.

The Challenger demonstrably reversed two of the Generator's opening calls. Sub-q 2 went from "THRESHOLD-SHIFTER" (Round 1, line 26) to "hybrid SLOPE-REDUCER + small THRESHOLD-SHIFT" (converged, line 59), with the Generator conceding the original "encoded a fiction the corpus rejects" (line 40) under May/Karasulas/Conlan pushback — changing the flag encoding to a two-field struct {slope_multiplier, threshold_offset} where slope-reduction is dominant. The Generator's fourth-axis proposal to "abstract intra-gait phase into the trained-mount flag" (line 27) was WITHDRAWN as "dishonest" (lines 42, 50) and replaced with an explicit deferred limit naming the real cost (gait_phase field + phase-aware interpolation). The Challenger also surfaced a fifth axis (draw-cycle/gait-cycle coupling) that neither Generator nor the originating question named (line 32), which became item 5 of the converged shape, plus a clamp on threshold_offset (parent recommendation item 3). These are substantive before/after differences in the architecture the parent is told to adopt, driven by the Challenger's independent biomechanics corpus — not caveats or polish. Note: as a single committed artifact it cannot be independently confirmed the debate wasn't self-authored, but within its own documented trace a sub-question call was genuinely flipped.

The converged recommendation (Option 3 Mixed register, governed by the grounding_tier selector) is identical to the Generator's Round 2 proposal, which the Generator reached independently after reading the decision pack. The Challenger explicitly agreed "Option 3 is correct" and its contribution is three "unresolved at cap" items, all flagged as partner to-dos rather than decision changes: (1) Spiritualia within-tier variance — "recommend partner consultation with Idris"; (2) LLM-grounding asymmetry — "recommend explicit framing in the rule's documentation"; (3) sci-fi entities — the default "works, but should be stated explicitly rather than deferred." None reverses the call, replaces the approach, or drops/adds an option family. The artifact itself concedes the point: "The Generator-Challenger disagreement at cap is on operational completeness, not on the chosen option." The strongest TRUE candidate (Spiritualia flattening) was punted to a future partner consultation, not resolved — the grounding_tier rule ships unchanged with an annotation. These are caveats and refinements that do not change what the parent should DO.

The converged call is identical to the Generator's opening: "team members" as the single primary handbook term, reject "colleagues." The Challenger reversed nothing and added/dropped no option family. Its two contributions are non-load-bearing: (1) It expanded the legal remedy from "a single definitional clause" to a "two-layer legal anchor (first-use definition + 'employees (team members)' pairing in at-will/termination sections)" — but it explicitly labels this "belt-and-suspenders" (line 22), i.e., redundant extra robustness on a remedy the Generator already had, not a defect that would have shipped. The ceremony's own summary calls it "tightened a real weakness... glossed" — refinement language, not an error caught. (2) It reframed the survey from "strong despite conformity pressure" to "plausibly a ceiling; corroborates rather than carries" — pure interpretive framing of evidence weight that does not change the term decision. Neither changes what the parent should DO about the actual address-term call.

The Challenger demonstrably changed the converged recommendation. Generator's opening (Round 1) was a hybrid with "concepts as the spine" and a per-week two-mode dual-frame handoff. The Challenger raised concrete operational blockers — the dual-frame hybrid is undeliverable across line-21 staffing (4 FT + 6-8 under-resourced adjuncts, ~8 sections) and the June 30 deadline can't absorb new week-by-week dual materials. The Generator conceded "as decisive" and "stepped down to Path B (language-first) + one bounded concept overlay" (fixed FT-authored sidebar + lab prompts on existing 2018 materials), explicitly rejecting the per-week dual-frame hybrid. The converged output is a different architecture/option family than the opening proposal — a real reversal. The Challenger additionally caught a specific interpretive error: the Generator read Evidence 6's 64→73% 2018 jump as evidence that early-syntax pedagogy drives completion, when it was a language change with no ordering contrast; the Generator withdrew that thread entirely.

The Generator opened with the "Pseudocode-Anchored Spiral" (PAS) hybrid: concept-primacy-leaning, weeks 1-2 in pseudocode + receptive trace-and-predict, generative projects beginning WEEK 4, and a 12-cycle weekly handoff among three modes. After the Challenger's five concrete concerns, the Generator explicitly "Replaced PAS with Modified Path B": generative from day one with a self-authored artifact by END OF WEEK 2, a SINGLE-mode project-based architecture (no weekly mode handoff), trace-and-predict demoted from framing mode to embedded 20-30 min construct-specific micro-passes, primary design shifted to the 62% terminal cohort, and the transfer cohort moved to a separate second-course bridge. This is a replaced approach and a reversed call on multiple load-bearing axes (week-4 → week-2 first generative milestone; three-mode → single-mode; concept-first-leaning → generative-first), driven by the Challenger's andragogy/community-college evidence base (Tinto integration window, Wlodkowski/Ginsberg, Rendón validation, Eagan & Jaeger adjunct-consistency, Bailey majority-population). The Generator conceded "all five concerns." This is substance, not wording.

The headline decision (vocabulary = "team members") never changed — both agents agreed from the start and the ceremony concedes the disputes are about "completeness of the implementation enumeration." Most Challenger additions (extra hybrid sections, expanded Section 7 list, typographic specs) are non-load-bearing enumeration polish. BUT the Challenger caught a specific, concrete legal defect in the Generator's affirmatively-drafted output: the opening front-matter clause merely "link[ed] the cultural register to the legal-classification term" (terminology equivalence only). The Challenger flagged that this "does not foreclose the Toussaint-line implied-promise reading" and supplied replacement language integrating equivalence + at-will + non-modification. The converged deliverable adopted the fix: the parent recommendation now requires a clause that "explicitly integrates terminology equivalence with the at-will employment relationship, not merely terminology equivalence alone." An equivalence-only front-matter clause lacking an at-will disclaimer is a genuine implied-contract exposure (Toussaint/Woolley) that erodes at-will employment, and the management-scientist Generator drafted and would have shipped the defective version. This is a real legal risk corrected in the substance of the deliverable, not framing.
